header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Meraki MS130 and MS150 Series switches (native Meraki cloud-managed access switches — distinct from the Catalyst 9200L-M/9300L-M hybrid Meraki-managed models, which live in their own C9200/C9300 KB files). Carries the catalog (atomic SKU facts), groups (named shared compatibility sets), and switch models for MS130 (compact access) and MS150 (stackable access). MS130 licensing catalog/groups and MS130 model entries (11: 8/8P/8P-I/8X/12X/24/24P/24X/48/48P/48X) are complete. MS150 is not yet started. Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco Meraki MS130 Series Data Sheet

design_notes: Pass 1: MS130 licensing — catalog.licenses + groups.license_groups, from MS130 datasheet excerpts pasted into the authoring session.

Pass 2 (this pass): 11 MS130 switch models added (8/8P/8P-I/8X/12X/24/24P/24X/48/48P/48X), from the two MS130 spec-table images/text pasted into the authoring session. No network_modules (fixed uplinks only, uplink_modular=false throughout) and no stack_cables/stackpower_cables (no stacking on this series, confirmed below). Four modeling decisions were confirmed with the user (AskUserQuestion) before authoring: (1) PoE type — every PoE model states 'PoE Type: 802.3bt' with a 30W per-port budget; mapped to registry poe_type 'poe+' since its level_watts (30W) matches exactly, rather than inventing an '802.3bt' enum value. (2) -X model ports — mGbe RJ45 ports (8X/12X/24X/48X) modeled as role=access, speeds=[1g,2.5g] (100M omitted, sub-1G isn't a tracked speed); 10GbE SFP+ ports modeled as role=uplink, speeds=[1g,10g] (dual-rate, matching how other SFP+ uplinks are modeled elsewhere in this project). (3) Stacking — no stacking row appears anywhere in the pasted MS130 tables; confirmed as stacking_capable=false/stackpower_capable=false for all 11 models, with no PSU redundancy either (single fixed PSU only, no secondary/redundant option on any model). (4) PSU wattage — no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage is given anywhere in the tables (only 'Power Input' voltage x current, and 'Power Load idle/max'); confirmed approach is to use each model's own Power Load (max) figure as a conservative proxy for the SYNTHETIC catalog PSU's watts field (same synthetic-placeholder convention as C9200CX's fixed/internal supplies), flagged _incomplete:['power_supply_watts'] on every model and tracked in THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md pending a real nameplate rating.

The 24/48-series models' 'Dedicated Mgmt Interface' row is recorded as attributes.dedicated_mgmt_interface (boolean) rather than a ports[] row, since the registry's port_role enum only has 'access'/'uplink' — an out-of-band management port isn't a registered role, so per this project's own axis/variable/attribute trichotomy it is a display-only attribute, excluded from total_port_count.

'MS130' and 'MS150' were added to the 'series' enum in switching-axes.json with explicit human sign-off (structural change, per this schema's $authoring.cheap_vs_structural). No registry_version bump was made for this addition since no axis KIND/ROLE/shape changed — only its legal_values grew, matching how registry_version is scoped in this project (a bump is for a structural/typed change to the axis SET, not every enum-value addition).

MS130 classic license structure (regime meraki-classic, shape term-based): the datasheet's classic table prints the LIC-MS130-CMPT(A)/-24(A)/-48(A) base order codes each against ONE representative model row, but the 'compact switch (8/12)' / '24 ports' / '48 ports' wording in the Description column states each SKU applies to the WHOLE port-class family, not just the row it's printed on. Confirmed with the user: 'compact' = MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X (both Enterprise and Advanced SKUs available across all five); '24' = MS130-24/-24P/-24X; '48' = MS130-48/-48P/-48X. Per-term SKU strings (-1Y/-3Y/.../-10Y) are synthesized by appending suffixes to the datasheet's base order code (datasheet prints only the base code + a stated term list), mirroring the C9200L-M convention. Enterprise offers 1/3/5/7/10-year terms; Advanced offers 1/3/5-year terms only (no 7/10y) — confirmed uniform across all three port classes.

MS130 subscription license structure (regime meraki-subscription, shape subscription-based, term_years null): LIC-MS-100-{S,M,L}-{E,A}, tier-split per the C9200L-M LIC-MS-200-{M,L}-{E,A} convention. MS100 Small = compact class (8/12-port models); MS100 Medium = 24-port class; MS100 Large = 48-port class.

Tier vocabulary: MS130's own tier names are Enterprise/Advanced; mapped to the registry's existing essentials/advantage legal_values (Enterprise->essentials, Advanced->advantage), same mapping convention used for C9200L-M. No new license_tier axis value.

port_class on the compact-class license catalog entries and groups is recorded as null (not 8), since that class spans two distinct port counts (8-port and 12-port models) and the schema's port_class field is a single integer-or-null.
A trailing 'MS120' line at the end of the user's pasted subscription table was confirmed to be a paste artifact (not MS130 data) and is not reflected anywhere in this file.

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

power_supplies.notes: SYNTHETIC catalog placeholder — Cisco/Meraki does not sell this as an orderable SKU. MS130-8 datasheet gives no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage, only 'Power Input: 12VDC, 2.5A' and 'Power Load (idle/max): 8W/8W'. watts is a proxy = this model's own stated Power Load (max), NOT an independently disclosed PSU rating (confirmed approach; see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md). Fixed external power brick, not field-replaceable/orderable separately.

SYNTHETIC catalog placeholder — not a real Cisco/Meraki SKU. MS130-8P datasheet gives no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage, only 'Power Input: 54VDC, 2.78A' and 'Power Load (idle/max): 8W/128W'. watts is a proxy = this model's own stated Power Load (max) (see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md). Fixed external power brick.

SYNTHETIC catalog placeholder — not a real Cisco/Meraki SKU. MS130-8P-I datasheet gives no PSU part number or nameplate wattage, only 'Power Input: 100-240V~, 2.8A-1.2A, 50-60Hz' and 'Power Load (idle/max): 8W/128W'. watts is a proxy = this model's own stated Power Load (max) (see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md). Fixed internal (non-removable) AC supply, distinct from the DC-brick-powered 8P.

licenses.notes: Term suffix synthesized (1-year); datasheet prints only base order code LIC-MS130-CMPT (MS130 classic license table, Enterprise tier, 'compact switch (8/12)' row) and states Enterprise is offered in 1/3/5/7/10-year terms. Covers MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X (shared SKU family spanning both 8- and 12-port compact models; port_class recorded as null).

Covers MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X (shared SKU family spanning both 8- and 12-port compact models; port_class recorded as null).

Tier suffix synthesized (Essentials); datasheet prints only base order code 'MS100 Small' / LIC-MS-100-S covering the compact class (MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X), per the C9200L-M LIC-MS-200-M-E/-A convention.

Tier suffix synthesized (Advantage); datasheet prints only base order code 'MS100 Small' / LIC-MS-100-S covering the compact class (MS130-8/-8P/-8P-I/-8X/-12X), per the C9200L-M LIC-MS-200-M-E/-A convention.

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility sets referencing catalog SKUs by id.

power_supply_groups.notes: Fixed external power brick on MS130-8. Single-member group: no PSU bay, no FRU, no redundancy pair — exists only so the standard power_supplies shape applies (same rationale as the C9200CX single-member PSU groups).
Fixed external power brick on MS130-8P. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal AC supply on MS130-8P-I. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed external power brick on MS130-8X. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed external power brick on MS130-12X. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-24. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-24P. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-24X. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-48. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-48P. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.
Fixed internal supply on MS130-48X. Single-member group, same rationale as ms130-8-psu.

license_groups.applies_to: MS130-8, MS130-8P, MS130-8P-I, MS130-8X, MS130-12X (compact class, Enterprise tier, LIC-MS130-CMPT) (MS130 Datasheet, classic license table).

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis); the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU. No perpetual option. Per-term SKUs synthesized (datasheet prints only the base order code LIC-MS130-CMPT and states 1/3/5/7/10-year terms are offered for Enterprise).

models.description: Cisco Meraki MS130-8, native cloud-managed access switch. 8-port class, non-PoE, external power, fanless. No stacking.

axis_value_provenance.ports: MS130 Datasheet spec table: access = 8x copper [1g] (RJ45), uplink = 2x fiber [1g] (SFP). total_port_count = access sum = 8.
axis_value_provenance.poe_*: MS130 Datasheet: 'PoE Type: -' (no PoE row value). poe_capable=false, poe_type='none'.
axis_value_provenance.stacking: No stacking row appears anywhere in the MS130 datasheet excerpts provided; confirmed with user as 'no stacking' for this series. stacking_capable=false, stackpower_capable=false; no PSU redundancy either (single fixed PSU, no secondary/redundant option).
axis_value_provenance.power_supply_watts: SYNTHETIC placeholder (MS130-8-PSU-SYN): no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage is given in the datasheet. watts = this model's own stated Power Load (max) = 8W, used as a conservative proxy per confirmed approach (flagged _incomplete; see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md).
axis_value_provenance.weight_kg: MS130 Datasheet Weight row (kg figure used verbatim, no conversion).

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: MS130 Datasheet: single fixed PSU (synthetic), no PoE (data model). Empty matrix; no secondary/redundant PSU option on this model.

included_by_default_non_selectable.power_supply: Fixed external power adapter/brick (not field-replaceable, not user-swappable for another wattage).

models.description: Cisco Meraki MS130-8P, native cloud-managed access switch. 8-port class, PoE+ (802.3bt, 30W/port), external power, fanless. No stacking.

axis_value_provenance.ports: MS130 Datasheet spec table: access = 8x copper [1g] (RJ45), uplink = 2x fiber [1g] (SFP). total_port_count = access sum = 8.
axis_value_provenance.poe_*: MS130 Datasheet: 'PoE Type: 802.3bt', 'PoE Port Budget: 30W', 'PoE Switch Budget: 120W'. poe_type mapped to registry 'poe+' (level_watts 30W matches the stated port budget exactly); poe_budget_watts = stated PoE Switch Budget.
axis_value_provenance.stacking: No stacking row appears anywhere in the MS130 datasheet excerpts provided; confirmed with user as 'no stacking' for this series. stacking_capable=false, stackpower_capable=false; no PSU redundancy either (single fixed PSU, no secondary/redundant option).
axis_value_provenance.power_supply_watts: SYNTHETIC placeholder (MS130-8P-PSU-SYN): no PSU/adapter part number or nameplate wattage is given in the datasheet. watts = this model's own stated Power Load (max) = 128W, used as a conservative proxy per confirmed approach (flagged _incomplete; see THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md).
axis_value_provenance.weight_kg: MS130 Datasheet Weight row (kg figure used verbatim, no conversion).

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: MS130 Datasheet: single fixed PSU (MS130-8P-PSU-SYN, synthetic), PoE Switch Budget = 120W. No secondary/redundant PSU option on this model.

included_by_default_non_selectable.power_supply: Fixed external power adapter/brick (not field-replaceable, not user-swappable for another wattage).
